devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । कस्य हेतोः परित्यक्ता आचार्येणात्मनः सुराः । एतदाचक्ष्व भगवञ्छिष्याणामक्रमं गुरौ ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācakasya hetoḥ parityaktā ācāryeṇātmanaḥ surāḥetadācakṣva bhagavañchiṣyāṇāmakramaṃ gurau
translation:
The King said:
Please-tell me, Oh venerable sir, why the gods were forsaken by their own preceptor and what was the fault of the disciples (gods) against their teacher.

devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । इन्द्र स्त्रिभुवनैश्वर्य मदोल्लङ्घितसत्पथः । मरुद्भिर्वसुभी रुद्रैरादित्यैरृभुभिर्नृप ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvācaindra stribhuvanaiśvarya madollaṅghitasatpathaḥmarudbhirvasubhī rudrairādityairṛbhubhirnṛpa
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Indra transgressed the path followed by the good, due to his arrogance of overlordship of the three worlds. (Once) surrounded by Maruts, Vasu, Rudras, Ādityas, Ṛbhus, Viśvedevas, Sādhyas and the Aśvinikumāras, and attended upon and praised and sweetly extolled by Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Gandharvas, by sages, the knowers of Brahman, Vidyādharas, heavenly damsels, Kinnaras, birds and Nāgas (serpents), he (Indra) was seated on the throne equipped with a white umbrella, beautiful like the orb of the full Moon, and with chowries, fans and other insignia of supreme royalty. He looked extremely splendid with (his wife) Śacī occupying half the throne.

devanagari: विश्वेदेवैश्च साध्यैश्च नासत्याभ्यां परिश्रितः । सिद्धचारणगन्धर्वैर्मुनिभिर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: viśvedevaiśca sādhyaiśca nāsatyābhyāṃ pariśritaḥsiddhacāraṇagandharvairmunibhirbrahmavādibhiḥ
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: विद्याधराप्सरोभिश्च किन्नरैः पतगोरगैः । निषेव्यमाणो मघवान्स्तूयमानश्च भारत ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyādharāpsarobhiśca kinnaraiḥ patagoragaiḥniṣevyamāṇo maghavānstūyamānaśca bhārata
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: उपगीयमानो ललितमास्थानाध्यासनाश्रितः । पाण्डुरेणातपत्रेण चन्द्रमण्डलचारुणा ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamāno lalitamāsthānādhyāsanāśritaḥpāṇḍureṇātapatreṇa candramaṇḍalacāruṇā
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: युक्तश्चान्यैः पारमेष्ठ्यैश्चामरव्यजनादिभिः । विराजमानः पौलोम्या सहार्धासनया भृशम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yuktaścānyaiḥ pārameṣṭhyaiścāmaravyajanādibhiḥvirājamānaḥ paulomyā sahārdhāsanayā bhṛśam
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: स यदा परमाचार्यं देवानामात्मनश्च ह । नाभ्यनन्दत सम्प्राप्तं प्रत्युत्थानासनादिभिः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yadā paramācāryaṃ devānāmātmanaśca hanābhyanandata samprāptaṃ pratyutthānāsanādibhiḥ
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: वाचस्पतिं मुनिवरं सुरासुरनमस्कृतम् । नोच्चचालासनादिन्द्रः पश्यन्नपि सभागतम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vācaspatiṃ munivaraṃ surāsuranamaskṛtamnoccacālāsanādindraḥ paśyannapi sabhāgatam
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: ततो निर्गत्य सहसा कविराङ्गिरसः प्रभुः । आययौ स्वगृहं तूष्णीं विद्वान्श्रीमदविक्रियाम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tato nirgatya sahasā kavirāṅgirasaḥ prabhuḥāyayau svagṛhaṃ tūṣṇīṃ vidvānśrīmadavikriyām
translation: When he did not welcome the great preceptor of gods and of himself, by offering him due formalities of respect such as standing up, offering a seat, on his arrival; he (Indra) did not rise at all from his seat, even though he saw the great sage Bṛhaspati who was adorable both to gods and demons, coming to his assembly; the wise and powerful descendent of Aṅgiras (Bṛhaspati) who understood that the violation of good manners is due to the arrogance of affluence and power, came out of the assembly and quietly returned to his house.

devanagari: तर्ह्येव प्रतिबुध्येन्द्रो गुरुहेलनमात्मनः । गर्हयामास सदसि स्वयमात्मानमात्मना ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tarhyeva pratibudhyendro guruhelanamātmanaḥgarhayāmāsa sadasi svayamātmānamātmanā
translation: At that very moment, Indra became aware of the disrespect shown by him to his preceptor, and he, of his own accord, censured himself publicly in the assembly.

devanagari: अहो बत मयासाधु कृतं वै दभ्रबुद्धिना । यन्मयैश्वर्यमत्तेन गुरुः सदसि कात्कृतः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: aho bata mayāsādhu kṛtaṃ vai dabhrabuddhināyanmayaiśvaryamattena guruḥ sadasi kātkṛtaḥ
translation: “Oh what a pity! What an unrighteous and wicked deed has been committed by me, of poor intellect: being intoxicated with the arrogance of wealth and power, my preceptor has been disrespectfully treated in this assembly.

devanagari: को गृध्येत्पण्डितो लक्ष्मीं त्रिपिष्टपपतेरपि । ययाहमासुरं भावं नीतोऽद्य विबुधेश्वरः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ko gṛdhyetpaṇḍito lakṣmīṃ tripiṣṭapapaterapiyayāhamāsuraṃ bhāvaṃ nīto'dya vibudheśvaraḥ
translation: Who will covet for the prosperity and wealth of the Lord of the celestial regions, if he be wise? for I who am the Lord of gods of sāttvic nature was dragged into the demonic state of egotism by that prosperity.

devanagari: यः पारमेष्ठ्यं धिषणमधितिष्ठन्न कञ्चन । प्रत्युत्तिष्ठेदिति ब्रूयुर्धर्मं ते न परं विदुः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ pārameṣṭhyaṃ dhiṣaṇamadhitiṣṭhanna kañcanapratyuttiṣṭhediti brūyurdharmaṃ te na paraṃ viduḥ
translation: . Those who lay down that the occupants of the highest position should not rise to receive anyone, do not know the highest code of conduct.

devanagari: तेषां कुपथदेष्टॄणां पततां तमसि ह्यधः । ये श्रद्दध्युर्वचस्ते वै मज्जन्त्यश्मप्लवा इव ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ kupathadeṣṭṝṇāṃ patatāṃ tamasi hyadhaḥye śraddadhyurvacaste vai majjantyaśmaplavā iva
translation: Those who believe in the words of those who show the wrong course and themselves sink in the dark infernal regions, verily, go down (to the abysmal depth of hell) by boarding a raft of granite rock, as it were.

devanagari: अथाहममराचार्यमगाधधिषणं द्विजम् । प्रसादयिष्ये निशठः शीर्ष्णा तच्चरणं स्पृशन् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: athāhamamarācāryamagādhadhiṣaṇaṃ dvijamprasādayiṣye niśaṭhaḥ śīrṣṇā taccaraṇaṃ spṛśan
translation: Now with all my crookedness shed off and touching with my head the feet of that Brāhmaṇa, the preceptor of gods, and possessor of unfathomable intelligence, I shall propitiate him.”

devanagari: एवं चिन्तयतस्तस्य मघोनो भगवान्गृहात् । बृहस्पतिर्गतोऽदृष्टां गतिमध्यात्ममायया ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ cintayatastasya maghono bhagavāngṛhātbṛhaspatirgato'dṛṣṭāṃ gatimadhyātmamāyayā
translation: While Indra was musing thus, the venerable Bṛhaspati went out of his mansion and disappeared with his superb spiritual power.

devanagari: गुरोर्नाधिगतः संज्ञां परीक्षन्भगवान्स्वराट् । ध्यायन्धिया सुरैर्युक्तः शर्म नालभतात्मनः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: gurornādhigataḥ saṃjñāṃ parīkṣanbhagavānsvarāṭdhyāyandhiyā surairyuktaḥ śarma nālabhatātmanaḥ
translation: The glorious Lord of the celestial region, looked on all sides for the whereabouts of his preceptor, but could not trace him. Anxiously considering (what will happen to them all, in the absence of the protection of the preceptor) Indra, though he commanded the loyalty of all gods, did not get the peace of mind.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वैवासुराः सर्व आश्रित्यौशनसं मतम् । देवान्प्रत्युद्यमं चक्रुर्दुर्मदा आततायिनः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvaivāsurāḥ sarva āśrityauśanasaṃ matamdevānpratyudyamaṃ cakrurdurmadā ātatāyinaḥ
translation: As soon as all the demons got the report of it (the desertion of gods’ side by Bṛhaspati), they followed the advice of Śukra. Haughty as they were, they carried a war against gods with their arms raised.

devanagari: तैर्विसृष्टेषुभिस्तीक्ष्णैर्निर्भिन्नाङ्गोरुबाहवः । ब्रह्माणं शरणं जग्मुः सहेन्द्रा नतकन्धराः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tairvisṛṣṭeṣubhistīkṣṇairnirbhinnāṅgorubāhavaḥbrahmāṇaṃ śaraṇaṃ jagmuḥ sahendrā natakandharāḥ
translation: Gods whose heads, thighs and arms were deeply- wounded by the sharp arrows discharged by the demons, went along with Indra, with their heads lowered in humility, to god Brahmā for protection.

devanagari: तांस्तथाभ्यर्दितान्वीक्ष्य भगवानात्मभूरजः । कृपया परया देव उवाच परिसान्त्वयन् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃstathābhyarditānvīkṣya bhagavānātmabhūrajaḥkṛpayā parayā deva uvāca parisāntvayan
translation: The venerable birthless self-born god Brahmā saw them afflicted and harassed that way. Consoling them, the god spoke out to them in his supreme grace.

devanagari: श्रीब्रह्मोवाच । अहो बत सुरश्रेष्ठा ह्यभद्रं वः कृतं महत् । ब्रह्मिष्ठं ब्राह्मणं दान्तमैश्वर्यान्नाभ्यनन्दत ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrahmovācaaho bata suraśreṣṭhā hyabhadraṃ vaḥ kṛtaṃ mahatbrahmiṣṭhaṃ brāhmaṇaṃ dāntamaiśvaryānnābhyanandata
translation:
God Brahmā said:
What a pity it is, Oh great gods! Out of arrogance of power and wealth you have certainly committed a very unjust and inauspicious deed in not respectfully welcoming a self-controlled Brāhmaṇa who was imbued with the spirit of Brahman.

devanagari: तस्यायमनयस्यासीत्परेभ्यो वः पराभवः । प्रक्षीणेभ्यः स्ववैरिभ्यः समृद्धानां च यत्सुराः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāyamanayasyāsītparebhyo vaḥ parābhavaḥprakṣīṇebhyaḥ svavairibhyaḥ samṛddhānāṃ ca yatsurāḥ
translation: It is the result of your unrighteousness that you gods, rich and powerful as you are, are defeated at the hands of your enemies who were extremely weak.

devanagari: मघवन्द्विषतः पश्य प्रक्षीणान्गुर्वतिक्रमात् । सम्प्रत्युपचितान्भूयः काव्यमाराध्य भक्तितः । आददीरन्निलयनं ममापि भृगुदेवताः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: maghavandviṣataḥ paśya prakṣīṇāngurvatikramātsampratyupacitānbhūyaḥ kāvyamārādhya bhaktitaḥādadīrannilayanaṃ mamāpi bhṛgudevatāḥ
translation: Oh Indra! Look at your enemies who became extremely powerless due to disrespect shown by them to their preceptor, have again grown in strength due to their devoted propitiation of their preceptor Śukra. They, who regard Śukra as their deity, may capture even my abode (Brahma- Ioka).

devanagari: त्रिपिष्टपं किं गणयन्त्यभेद्य मन्त्रा भृगूणामनुशिक्षितार्थाः । न विप्रगोविन्दगवीश्वराणां भवन्त्यभद्राणि नरेश्वराणाम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tripiṣṭapaṃ kiṃ gaṇayantyabhedya mantrā bhṛgūṇāmanuśikṣitārthāḥna vipragovindagavīśvarāṇāṃ bhavantyabhadrāṇi nareśvarāṇām
translation: The demons who are well instructed in their objectives by Śukra and whose secret designs are not divulged, have the slightest regard for the celestial region. (They can easily conquer it). Inauspicious results do not befall kings who are protected by Brāhmaṇas, Lord Viṣṇu and cows.

devanagari: तद्विश्वरूपं भजताशु विप्रं तपस्विनं त्वाष्ट्रमथात्मवन्तम् । सभाजितोऽर्थान्स विधास्यते वो यदि क्षमिष्यध्वमुतास्य कर्म ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tadviśvarūpaṃ bhajatāśu vipraṃ tapasvinaṃ tvāṣṭramathātmavantamsabhājito'rthānsa vidhāsyate vo yadi kṣamiṣyadhvamutāsya karma
translation: Therefore, take resort to Viśvarūpa immediately. That Brāhmaṇa, the son of Tvaṣṭṛ, possesses self-control and (a wealth of) penance. If respectfully treated by you, he will accomplish your objects, if you put up with (some of) his acts (of partiality to demons).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । त एवमुदिता राजन्ब्रह्मणा विगतज्वराः । ऋषिं त्वाष्ट्रमुपव्रज्य परिष्वज्येदमब्रुवन् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācata evamuditā rājanbrahmaṇā vigatajvarāḥṛṣiṃ tvāṣṭramupavrajya pariṣvajyedamabruvan
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh King! Gods who were thus advised by god Brahmā, were relieved of their feverish anxiety. They approached the sage Viśvarūpa, the son of Tvaṣṭṛ, embraced him and addressed him thus.

devanagari: श्रीदेवा ऊचुः । वयं तेऽतिथयः प्राप्ता आश्रमं भद्रमस्तु ते । कामः सम्पाद्यतां तात पितॄणां समयोचितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīdevā ūcuḥvayaṃ te'tithayaḥ prāptā āśramaṃ bhadramastu tekāmaḥ sampādyatāṃ tāta pitṝṇāṃ samayocitaḥ
translation:
Gods said:
We have approached your hermitage as unexpected guests. May you be blessed, Oh child. Please accomplish the wish of your elders (uncles) as demanded by the occasion.

devanagari: पुत्राणां हि परो धर्मः पितृशुश्रूषणं सताम् । अपि पुत्रवतां ब्रह्मन्किमुत ब्रह्मचारिणाम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: putrāṇāṃ hi paro dharmaḥ pitṛśuśrūṣaṇaṃ satāmapi putravatāṃ brahmankimuta brahmacāriṇām
translation: It is the highest duty of virtuous sons to serve their parents, even though the sons themselves have got children. It is much more so in the case of celibates, Oh Brahman.

devanagari: आचार्यो ब्रह्मणो मूर्तिः पिता मूर्तिः प्रजापतेः । भ्राता मरुत्पतेर्मूर्तिर्माता साक्षात्क्षितेस्तनुः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryo brahmaṇo mūrtiḥ pitā mūrtiḥ prajāpateḥbhrātā marutpatermūrtirmātā sākṣātkṣitestanuḥ
translation: The preceptor who invests the sacred thread and initiates in the Vedas, is the embodiment of the Vedas. Father is god Brahmā (the creator) incarnate. A brother is as if an image of Indra, the chief of gods. The mother is a veritable earth personified.

devanagari: दयाया भगिनी मूर्तिर्धर्मस्यात्मातिथिः स्वयम् । अग्नेरभ्यागतो मूर्तिः सर्वभूतानि चात्मनः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dayāyā bhaginī mūrtirdharmasyātmātithiḥ svayamagnerabhyāgato mūrtiḥ sarvabhūtāni cātmanaḥ
translation: A sister is the embodiment of tenderness. An unexpected visitor is the soul of dharma (the god of righteousness). And the guest staying with us is the god of Fire incarnate and all beings are the representations of the Supreme Soul of Lord Viṣṇu. (Therefore, one should look upon all beings like himself).

devanagari: तस्मात्पितॄणामार्तानामार्तिं परपराभवम् । तपसापनयंस्तात सन्देशं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātpitṝṇāmārtānāmārtiṃ paraparābhavamtapasāpanayaṃstāta sandeśaṃ kartumarhasi
translation: Oh child, you should carry out our request and remove by your penance the afflictions of us, your elders, who are suffering from defeat at the hands of our enemies.

devanagari: वृणीमहे त्वोपाध्यायं ब्रह्मिष्ठं ब्राह्मणं गुरुम् । यथाञ्जसा विजेष्यामः सपत्नांस्तव तेजसा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛṇīmahe tvopādhyāyaṃ brahmiṣṭhaṃ brāhmaṇaṃ gurumyathāñjasā vijeṣyāmaḥ sapatnāṃstava tejasā
translation: We select you, who are possessed of Brahmanic power, and a Brāhmaṇa ḍeserving respect, as our preceptor, so that we shall easily and thoroughly vanquish our enemies by the virtue of your spiritual power.

devanagari: न गर्हयन्ति ह्यर्थेषु यविष्ठाङ्घ्र्यभिवादनम् । छन्दोभ्योऽन्यत्र न ब्रह्मन्वयो ज्यैष्ठ्यस्य कारणम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: na garhayanti hyartheṣu yaviṣṭhāṅghryabhivādanamchandobhyo'nyatra na brahmanvayo jyaiṣṭhyasya kāraṇam
translation: They (the wise) do not censure the act of bowing at the feet of younger persons for the accomplishment of their objects. Mere age is not the ground of superiority when one is wanting in Vedic knowledge.

devanagari: श्रीऋषिरुवाच । अभ्यर्थितः सुरगणैः पौरहित्ये महातपाः । स विश्वरूपस्तानाह प्रसन्नः श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīṛṣiruvācaabhyarthitaḥ suragaṇaiḥ paurahitye mahātapāḥsa viśvarūpastānāha prasannaḥ ślakṣṇayā girā
translation:
The Sage (Śuka) said:
The great sage Viśvarūpa who was so earnestly entreated by the gods for accepting priesthood, became pleased and spoke to them in soft words.

devanagari: श्रीविश्वरूप उवाच । विगर्हितं धर्मशीलैर्ब्रह्मवर्चौपव्ययम् । कथं नु मद्विधो नाथा लोकेशैरभियाचितम् । प्रत्याख्यास्यति तच्छिष्यः स एव स्वा...
english_devnagari: śrīviśvarūpa uvācavigarhitaṃ dharmaśīlairbrahmavarcaupavyayamkathaṃ nu madvidho nāthā lokeśairabhiyācitampratyākhyāsyati tacchiṣyaḥ sa eva svārtha ucyate
translation:
Viśvarūpa said:
Priesthood has been condemned by the righteous ones as leading to the loss of spiritual power. But, Oh masters, how a person like me who deserves to be commanded by Lords of worlds like you, can refuse your request, for compliance (of your orders) is in my real interest.

devanagari: अकिञ्चनानां हि धनं शिलोञ्छनं तेनेह निर्वर्तितसाधुसत्क्रियः । कथं विगर्ह्यं नु करोम्यधीश्वराः पौरोधसं हृष्यति येन दुर्मतिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: akiñcanānāṃ hi dhanaṃ śiloñchanaṃ teneha nirvartitasādhusatkriyaḥkathaṃ vigarhyaṃ nu karomyadhīśvarāḥ paurodhasaṃ hṛṣyati yena durmatiḥ
translation: To those who decide not to possess anything, grain that is gleaned after the removal of corn from the fields and the grains of corn found after the market-day is over, form the wealth. I have performed my righteous duties with that wealth. Oh suzerine Lords, how should I accept the reproachful priesthood which pleases only those who are wretched in intellect.

devanagari: तथापि न प्रतिब्रूयां गुरुभिः प्रार्थितं कियत् । भवतां प्रार्थितं सर्वं प्राणैरर्थैश्च साधये ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpi na pratibrūyāṃ gurubhiḥ prārthitaṃ kiyatbhavatāṃ prārthitaṃ sarvaṃ prāṇairarthaiśca sādhaye
translation: I, however, do not refuse what has been requested by elders like you. Whatever insignificant is desired by you,. I shall accomplish it, at the cost of my life and means.

devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । तेभ्य एवं प्रतिश्रुत्य विश्वरूपो महातपाः । पौरोहित्यं वृतश्चक्रे परमेण समाधिना ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvācatebhya evaṃ pratiśrutya viśvarūpo mahātapāḥpaurohityaṃ vṛtaścakre parameṇa samādhinā
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The great ascetic Viśvarūpa who was thus solicited for accepting priesthood promised them (the gods) thus, and carried out his duties of priesthood, with perfect concentration.

devanagari: सुरद्विषां श्रियं गुप्तामौशनस्यापि विद्यया । आच्छिद्यादान्महेन्द्राय वैष्णव्या विद्यया विभुः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: suradviṣāṃ śriyaṃ guptāmauśanasyāpi vidyayāācchidyādānmahendrāya vaiṣṇavyā vidyayā vibhuḥ
translation: The (spiritually) powerful sage snatched the fortune of the enemies of gods even though it was protected by the incantations and spells of Śukra, and by the prayer (lore) of Viṣṇu, i.e. by Nārāyaṇa Kavaca, gave it to Indra.

devanagari: यया गुप्तः सहस्राक्षो जिग्येऽसुरचमूर्विभुः । तां प्राह स महेन्द्राय विश्वरूप उदारधीः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yayā guptaḥ sahasrākṣo jigye'suracamūrvibhuḥtāṃ prāha sa mahendrāya viśvarūpa udāradhīḥ
translation: The noble-minded Viśvarūpa initiated Mahendra in that lore, and Indra, the thousand-eyed god, became powerful and protected by that lore, vanquished the armies of demons.
